Regional Investment Thesis
Ayrshire is investable because it is fragmented
Ayrshire is not a single-market bet. It is a fragmented regional acquisition environment where the investor return profile depends heavily on town selection, property type, rent evidence quality, liquidity depth and execution risk. The region contains several distinct market types inside one geography: large-volume core towns, low-entry flat markets, high-yield value towns, selective BRRR locations and premium coastal capital-preservation markets.
⚠ The correct product is not a single "Ayrshire average". Use the town-by-town, property-type-specific intelligence layer in the BTL Matrix and town profiles.
Council Area Overview
Council AreaEst. Monthly SalesMajority TypeAvg PriceCore TownsInvestor ProfileConfidence
East Ayrshire ~180–220 Flats £163,281 Kilmarnock, Cumnock, Galston, Newmilns Value-led, affordability, BRRR-sensitive Moderate
North Ayrshire ~200–240 Flats £170,350 Irvine, Largs, Ardrossan, Saltcoats, Stevenston, Kilwinning, Dalry, Kilbirnie Investor-yield with coastal and commuter sub-markets Moderate
South Ayrshire ~160–200 Flats (Ayr/Prestwick) £160–175k Ayr, Prestwick, Troon, Maybole, Girvan Stronger liquidity in core/coastal; yield compression in premium Moderate
Town Liquidity Overview
Town / ClusterCouncilAvg PriceEst. Monthly SalesLiquidityInvestor Profile
AyrSouth£202,37370–110HighCore BTL, selective BRRR, strong rental market
PrestwickSouth£227,70125–45HighCapital preservation, premium BTL · +13% YoY
KilmarnockEast£159,22260–100HighAffordable core BTL, flat-led liquidity, selective BRRR
IrvineNorth£161,11040–70HighEmployment-led BTL, family tenant demand
LargsNorth~£170–190k20–40Med-HighCoastal capital preservation, downsizer demand
TroonSouth~£200k+15–35Med-HighPremium coastal BTL, capital preservation
Ardrossan / Saltcoats / StevenstonNorth£80–130k30–50 comb.MediumHigh-yield BTL, selective BRRR, affordable stock
KilwinningNorthMid-range15–30MediumFamily BTL, commuter demand, selective BRRR
Dalry / Kilbirnie / Galston / NewmilnsNorth / East£55–120k5–20 eachMed-LowHigh-yield BTL, verify evidence and close quality
Cumnock / Maybole / GirvanEast / SouthBelow £150k10–25Med-LowVerification-first / discounted-only markets
Market Segmentation by Investor Strategy
Opportunity RankSegmentBest TownsStrategyLiquidityYieldRisk
1Core BTL liquidityAyr, Kilmarnock, IrvineStandard BTL / selective BRRRHighMediumMedium
2Premium capital preservationPrestwick, Troon, LargsCapital preservation / premium BTLHigh–MedLow–MedMed-Low
3N. Ayrshire yield clusterArdrossan, Saltcoats, Stevenston, KilwinningHigh-yield BTL / selective BRRRMediumMed-HighMed-High
4Value-town flat yieldDalry, Kilbirnie, Galston, NewmilnsHigh-yield BTL / discounted-onlyMed-LowHigh–V.HighHigh
5Selective BRRRAyr, Stevenston, Galston, Cumnock, KilbirnieBRRR where GDV is evidencedMediumMed-HighMed-High
6Screening-grade marketsMaybole, Girvan, Newmilns, thin villagesWatchlist / discounted-onlyLow–MedMed–HighHigh
Houses vs Flats — Investment Analysis
Property TypeRoleStrengthsRisksBest-Fit Towns
1-Bed FlatHigh-yield / low-entry BTLLow price, high headline yieldThin demand, close/title risk, weaker tenant depthKilbirnie, Dalry, Galston, Kilmarnock
2-Bed FlatStandard / high-yield BTLBetter tenant flexibility, still affordableCommon repairs, rental evidence can be directionalAyr, Kilmarnock, Dalry, Galston, Largs
2-Bed HouseCore BTL / family tenant stabilityBetter tenant retention, broader demand, stronger resaleHigher entry, lower gross yield in premium townsAyr, Prestwick, Kilwinning, Beith, Dalry, Stevenston
Premium coastalCapital preservationLiquidity, lifestyle demand, resale strengthYield compression, holiday-let distortion, overpaying riskPrestwick, Troon, Largs, Ayr KA7
Regional Risk Register — Key Items
High-priority risks · Source: Phase 9 Risk Register
⚠
Thin town-level evidence — Newmilns, Maybole, Girvan, Beith flats. Do not scale acquisition until property-specific sold and rental comps are verified.
⚠
Flats misclassified as houses — verify title, property type and listing photos before modelling yield. Affects Ayr, Prestwick, North Ayrshire towns.
⚠
Holiday-let / short-term-let distortion — Girvan, Prestwick, Troon, Largs, Ayr coastal. Use only normal long-term rental evidence for BTL modelling.
⚠
Auction guide prices — Cumnock, Galston, Newmilns, Saltcoats. Mark as auction guide only until confirmed sale price is available.
⚠
Over-reliance on headline gross yield — low acquisition prices can make yields look exceptional in thin markets. Always weight confidence, liquidity, tenant demand and condition.
⚠
Rental category pages vs property-specific evidence — rental category pages should not be treated as confirmed rent comps. Replace with address-level rental comparables.

↑ Data Intake
Repeatable, safe process for adding new comparable evidence across all 64 towns and 3 property types
↑ Rightmove Listing Importer
Paste a Rightmove property URL — we extract the deal data and run an instant AI analysis
0.1% Quality Standard — Every entry must pass before integration
All new data must include: last_updated · confidence (0–100) · source_version. Active for-sale listings are labelled FOR SALE in sigLabel — never treated as confirmed sold comps. Auction prices are labelled AUCTION — DIRECTIONAL.
Update Log
All data integrations tracked here
DateTownProperty TypeEntries AddedSourceConfidence AvgStatus
Entry Template Generator
Generate the exact code block to paste into LIVE_DATA
Generated Entry Code

      


      
      

        
Scaling Process — All 64 Towns × 3 Property Types

      

      


        

          
Step-by-Step Workflow

          

            
1.Run the data refresh prompt for the target town + property type

            
2.Classify each entry: Confirmed Sold · For Sale (Active) · Auction

            
3.Use the Template Generator above to produce each entry's code block

            
4.Open the terminal HTML file · Find LIVE_DATA.townid['ptid'].sold

            
5.Append new entries immediately before the closing ],

            
6.Update kpis.comps count · Add refresh note to notes[]

            
✓Reload file in browser — verify town view renders correctly

          

        


        

          
Signal & Confidence Rules

          

            
STRONG BUYConfirmed sold comp in investor buy band · High confidence RoS evidence

            
BUYGood comp · Active listing at right price · Agent-confirmed evidence

            
WATCHDirectional · Auction · Aggregator source · Extended time window

            
PASSPremium outlier · Exclude from yield modelling entirely

            

              
Confidence Scoring:

              
90–100 = RoS confirmed, within 12mo, exact address

              
75–89 = Rightmove confirmed, within 18mo

              
60–74 = Directional / aggregator / asking price

              
Below 60 = Auction guide / historic / estimated

        All data integrated via this process must comply with the 0.1% quality standard: no auction guides used as market value, no asking prices labelled as sold comps, no category-page rents used as confirmed rent anchors. Every entry carries source, date, confidence and version metadata.

        🔨 Auction Intelligence
        RESEARCH SNAPSHOT
      

      
PPA + FPA lots · Manual source snapshot · 07 Jun 2026 · Verify current status on source sites before modelling or bidding


      
      

        

          ⚠️
          

            
IMPORTANT RISK WARNING — RESEARCH TOOL ONLY

            
Auction purchases in Scotland are legally binding on the fall of the hammer or acceptance of a pre-auction offer. You may lose your deposit if you cannot complete. All lots require independent legal advice, a full review of the legal pack, a commissioned RICS Home Report or structural survey (where not supplied), and verification of occupancy status, title, and any outstanding charges before bidding. This module is a research tool only and does not constitute financial, legal, or investment advice. Verify current status, prices, legal packs and deadlines on source sites before making any decision.
